ops.text:
## Lane: item 2 — prompt-cache restructure (static block -> system prompt) — COMPLETE. Recommendation: ADOPT. Cap $6, ~$5 notional spent.

Patch uncommitted in /Users/yasyf/Code/cc-steer-wfa (shared workspace). NOTHING shipped.

### SHAPE-CHANGE the brief got slightly wrong (resolved, not blocked)
Brief said "run_claude takes a system param" — but the triage/refine JUDGE path does NOT go through run_claude. It goes cc_steer.triage/refine -> cc_transcript.judge.structured_judge -> spawnllm.extract, and `extract` has NO system param; with no ClaudeConfig the claude backend emits `--system-prompt ""` and puts the ENTIRE static block on stdin per row. run_claude (which does take system) only feeds report.py + watcher/cascade.py, not the corpus judge. So I added `cached_judge()` in cc_steer/claude.py (a drop-in for structured_judge: builds RunSpec + ClaudeConfig(system_prompt=...) + response_model, calls spawnllm.run, raises JudgeError on failure) and routed triage/audit/refine through it. This is the faithful realization of the brief's intent.

### Patch (files, all mine)
- cc_steer/claude.py (+51): new `cached_judge[M](response_model, *, tier, system, timeout)`.
- cc_steer/triage.py: PROMPT_VERSION 6->7, AUDIT_VERSION 5->6; split JUDGE_PROMPT -> JUDGE_SYSTEM (7562 chars, static) + JUDGE_USER (91-char per-row template: [source]/{context}/{question_answer}/{text}); same for AUDIT_PROMPT -> AUDIT_SYSTEM/AUDIT_USER; triage()/audit() route through cached_judge(system=..._SYSTEM); dropped structured_judge import.
- cc_steer/refine.py: PROMPT_VERSION 3->4; split REFINE_PROMPT -> REFINE_SYSTEM/REFINE_USER (the per-row [judge's hint: {what_claude_did}] stays in USER); run_refinements routes through cached_judge.
- tests: monkeypatch targets cc_steer.{triage,refine}.structured_judge -> .cached_judge; build_prompt(JUDGE/AUDIT_PROMPT) -> (JUDGE/AUDIT_USER); regenerated golden tests/fixtures/build_prompt_2_0.txt (498 lines shorter — static block no longer in the per-row render). `uv run --no-sync pytest tests/test_triage.py tests/test_refine.py tests/test_enrich.py` all green.
- ADOPTION RE-JUDGES THE CORPUS: the version bumps (triage 6->7, audit 5->6, refine 3->4) invalidate every existing verdict/refinement — a full re-judge is required (loud: unjudged set jumps, all rows re-run). Semantics preserved (system+user concatenation == original JUDGE_PROMPT byte-for-byte; "below" references still resolve since user follows system).

### Evidence (sonnet/medium; distinct rows; fresh cp -c clone; frozen per-row context so OLD vs NEW differ ONLY in system-vs-user placement)
COST (14 distinct summary-fidelity rows = expired-transcript majority, the dominant corpus case):
- OLD (static-in-user): mean $0.0795/row.  NEW (static-in-system): mean $0.0543/row.  => NEW 0.683x OLD = 31.7% cheaper/row, cheaper on 13/14 rows.
- MECHANISM (usage envelopes): OLD cache_create ~8455 tok/row (static-in-user is NOT a stable cross-row cache prefix -> re-created each row at 1.25x); NEW cache_create ~3909, cache_read 39968 (static block hoisted to the CLI-cached system block -> cache-read at 0.1x). One-time cold system-cache write (warmup) = $0.035.
EQUIVALENCE (gate: old-vs-new match no worse than old-vs-old stochastic floor):
- Matched n=8 subset: old-vs-old category 0.750 (judge disagrees with ITSELF 2/8), accept 1.000; old-vs-new category 0.875, accept 1.000. => old-vs-new (0.875) >= old-vs-old floor (0.750) AND accept parity. GATE PASSES (new is even slightly ABOVE the noise floor).
- Full n=14: old-vs-new category 0.786, accept 0.929. The 3 category diffs are pure boundary noise: rows 2 & 13 shuffle WITHIN noise (operational_directive/status_update/new_task, accept unchanged); only row 14 flips accept (new_task<->wrong_approach, a known-hard boundary). No systematic directional shift — consistent with the 0.750 self-agreement floor.

### GATE: equivalence no worse (PASS) AND measured cost win (PASS ~32%) => ADOPT.

### Caveats / follow-ups for the orchestrator
1. Measured on summary-fidelity rows (majority case). Full-fidelity rows carry ~21-36K tok context (per item-1 lane), so the ~1.9K-token static block is a smaller fraction -> smaller RELATIVE win there (still positive; the pilot's one full-fidelity row also went NEW-cheaper).
2. BIGGER adjacent win, out of scope: every production judge call carries a ~35-40K-token Claude Code baseline (tools+system) because extract does NOT set tools=""/max_turns=1 (run_claude DOES). Stripping that baseline likely dwarfs this static-block win. Recommend a separate item to pass tools=""/max_turns=1 through the judge path (or extend extract).
3. n modest (14 cost / 8 matched equivalence) due to the $6 cap and aggressive per-call cost (~$0.05-0.08/summary row, up to $0.44 on a full-fidelity row). A larger confirm run (e.g. n=50, incl. full-fidelity) is cheap-ish if budget allows; the direction is unambiguous.
4. cached_judge is claude-backend-specific (ClaudeConfig only read by the claude backend) — matches run_claude's existing assumption and the claude-judged corpus; a non-claude backend would silently drop the system block. Fine for this env; flag if backends ever diverge.

Artifacts: /Users/yasyf/.cc-steer/experiments/WFA/scripts/{item2_measure,item2_controlled,item2_floor}.py, results/{item2_pilot,item2_controlled,item2_floor}.json.
